tools/migrate/cmd.purpose: Organisational parent for thin CLI entrypoints that operate on the migration toolchain. Separates CLI wiring from library logic — all business logic lives in sibling library packages under tools/migrate/; cmd/ sub-directories contain only flag parsing and delegation.

patterns.name: Thin entrypoint delegation
patterns.description: main.go parses flags and makes a single call into the sibling library package. Zero business logic in main. All generation, inspection, or transformation logic lives in the library.
patterns.example: viewgen.GenerateFile(*schemaPath, *outPath) — one library call, then exitf on error

patterns.name: stdlib flag for single-purpose tools
patterns.description: Single-command tools use the stdlib flag package. Cobra/Viper is reserved for multi-command binaries (cmd/server, cmd/jobs). Two flags need no sub-command routing.

patterns.name: exitf helper for error reporting
patterns.description: Errors are written to stderr via a local exitf helper and os.Exit(1). No log package, no panic.
patterns.example: func exitf(format string, args ...any) { fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, format, args...); os.Exit(1) }

patterns.name: One sub-command per directory
patterns.description: Each CLI tool gets its own cmd/<name>/ directory rather than adding multiple commands to a single main package.

key_file_guides.role: CLI entrypoint for ClickHouse view SQL generation: parses --schema and --out flags, delegates entirely to viewgen.GenerateFile, exits on error.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Do not add SQL generation or Ent schema inspection here — keep it in tools/migrate/viewgen. Do not import app/common or any openmeter domain package.

anti_patterns: Adding SQL generation or schema-inspection logic directly in main.go instead of the sibling library package
Importing app/common or any openmeter/ domain package — this binary only needs the schema path and the library
Using Cobra or Viper for a single-purpose tool that only needs one or two flags
Adding a second command to an existing cmd/<name>/ package instead of creating a sibling cmd/<othername>/ directory
Using log.Fatal or panic instead of a local exitf helper for error exits

decisions.decision: Library logic lives in tools/migrate/viewgen, not in main.go
decisions.rationale: Keeps the library testable and importable without invoking os.Exit; the cmd layer is purely a thin CLI shim.
A tool with two flags needs no sub-command routing; Cobra adds unnecessary import weight and ceremony for single-purpose migration tools.

code_examples.scenario: Adding a new single-purpose migration tool under tools/migrate/cmd/
